g.data-narration: Welcome to this comprehensive lecture on Water-Borne and Water-Induced Diseases - critical topics for engineering students.
Water-related diseases affect 2 billion people worldwide, causing 485,000 deaths annually from diarrheal diseases alone.
Water-borne diseases spread through contaminated water ingestion, while water-induced diseases spread via mosquito vectors breeding in water.
g.text: Caused by pathogens in contaminated water

g.data-narration: Water-related diseases are classified into four categories: water-borne, water-washed, water-based, and water-vector diseases.
Water-borne diseases spread through the fecal-oral route, with pathogens like Vibrio cholerae and Salmonella typhi contaminating water sources.
Water-washed diseases occur due to lack of water for hygiene, while water-based diseases involve organisms requiring water in their life cycle.
g.text: Poor hygiene due to water scarcity
Organisms need water in life cycle
Parasitic flukes breed in freshwater snails

g.data-narration: Water-vector diseases are transmitted by mosquitoes breeding in stagnant water - including malaria, dengue, and filariasis.
The fecal-oral transmission cycle shows how pathogens from infected feces contaminate water sources and spread to healthy individuals.
For water-induced diseases, mosquitoes breed in stagnant water, then bite humans to transmit pathogens like Plasmodium parasites.
Key water quality parameters include pH 6.5-8.5, zero coliforms per 100ml, turbidity, dissolved oxygen, and biochemical oxygen demand.
Cholera outbreak: Vibrio cholerae causes severe watery diarrhea. Treatment includes ORS and IV fluids; prevention requires water chlorination.
Malaria control involves draining stagnant water, using insecticide-treated nets, and applying larvicides to breeding sites.
g.text: Example 2: Malaria Control in a Village

g.data-narration: Typhoid fever caused by Salmonella typhi is treated with antibiotics and prevented through vaccination and safe food/water practices.
Water-borne diseases require ingestion of contaminated water, while water-induced diseases need mosquito vectors breeding in water.
g.text: Water-Induced: Vector breeding in stagnant water

g.data-narration: India's NVBDCP and Swachh Bharat Mission tackle water-related diseases through vector control and improved sanitation.
g.text: Prevention: Water treatment • Sanitation • Vector control • Vaccination • Public awareness
